channel.title: Claude AI Daily Brief
channel.description: Daily intelligence report on Claude AI news, Anthropic announcements, developer updates, and community buzz.

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 12, 2026
item.description:
1. Claude Cowork Goes GA With Enterprise Controls — Cowork exits preview and ships to all paid plans on macOS and Windows with RBAC, analytics, OpenTelemetry, and spend limits.

2. "Claude Mania" Sweeps HumanX Conference — 6,500 attendees and one consensus: Anthropic has stolen the spotlight from OpenAI. Claude Code was the tool on everyone's lips.

3. Claude Code 2.1.101 Ships Team Onboarding — New /team-onboarding command, auto-trust for OS CA certificates, and smarter remote-session setup.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 11, 2026
item.description:
1. Bessent & Powell Summon Bank CEOs to Emergency Mythos Meeting — Treasury Secretary and Fed Chair convened the heads of America's largest banks to discuss cybersecurity risks posed by Anthropic's Mythos model.

2. Software Stocks Slide 2.6% as Mythos Reignites Disruption Fears — S&P 500 Software Index extends its 2026 decline to 25.5% YTD. Cybersecurity names CrowdStrike, Cloudflare, and Okta fell 5-6.5%.

3. Claude Haiku 3 Retires April 19 — Eight days left to migrate from the original budget model. API calls to claude-3-haiku-20240307 will fail after the deadline.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 10, 2026
item.description:
1. Anthropic Signs Multi-Year CoreWeave Deal to Scale GPU Infrastructure — Bloomberg broke the story: Anthropic is renting data center capacity from CoreWeave across multiple Nvidia chip architectures to keep Claude running at production scale.

2. Anthropic Revenue Hits $30B Annualized Run Rate — Up more than 3x from $9B at year-end 2025, with enterprise contracts making up roughly 80% of the business.

3. Advisor Tool Launches in Public Beta — New API feature pairs a fast executor model with Opus 4.6 as a mid-generation advisor for long-horizon agentic tasks.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 9, 2026
item.description:
1. Claude Managed Agents Launches in Public Beta — Anthropic's new cloud-hosted agent runtime lets enterprises deploy Claude agents without managing infrastructure. Notion, Rakuten, Asana, Vibecode, and Sentry are early adopters.

2. Accenture Signs Multi-Year Partnership With Anthropic — The consulting giant will stand up a dedicated Claude practice and train 30,000 consultants on Anthropic's agentic platform, targeting Fortune 500 rollouts.

3. Snowflake Expands Partnership With $200M Commitment — The expanded deal embeds Claude directly in Snowflake for 12,600+ customers and launches a joint go-to-market for agentic workloads across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 8, 2026
item.description:
1. Project Glasswing Launches With $100M to Secure Critical Software — Anthropic deployed Claude Mythos Preview to find thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities across major OSes and browsers, then launched a coalition with Apple, AWS, Microsoft, and Google to fix them.

2. Claude Goes Down for Third Consecutive Day — Users reported login failures, chat errors, and degraded performance on April 8, less than 24 hours after the previous major outage.

3. Anthropic Revenue Run Rate Passes $30 Billion — Bloomberg reports annualized revenue has tripled since end of 2025, with over 1,000 enterprise customers spending $1M+ annually.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 7, 2026
item.description:
1. Anthropic Drops $400M on Biotech Startup Coefficient Bio — The acquisition brings computational drug discovery experts from Genentech into Anthropic's healthcare group to extend Claude for Life Sciences with molecular biology capabilities.

2. Claude Mythos Enters Limited Early Access With Cybersecurity Warning — Anthropic confirmed its most powerful model yet is being tested with enterprise partners, flagging "unprecedented cybersecurity risks" after an accidental source code leak.

3. OpenClaw Fallout Continues as Devs Scramble for Alternatives — Anthropic's subscription ban on third-party harnesses has sparked community backlash and a migration wave, with OpenClaw's open-source fork seeing a surge in GitHub stars.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 6, 2026
item.description:
1. Pentagon Appeals Ruling That Blocked Anthropic Blacklist — The Dept of War challenged the court order blocking Anthropic's supply-chain risk designation — the first ever applied to a US company under a law meant for foreign threats.

2. UK Moves to Woo Anthropic Amid US Defense Clash — Britain is pitching Anthropic on a London expansion and dual LSE listing. The GOV.UK AI assistant, already powered by Claude, anchors the offer.

3. Claude API Gets 300k Token Batches, Capability Fields, Data Residency — A wave of API improvements landed this week, plus model retirements for Sonnet 3.7 and Haiku 3.5. The 1M token beta ends April 30.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 5, 2026
item.description:
1. Claude Mythos Leak Reveals Anthropic's Next Frontier Model — A data leak exposed "Claude Mythos," described as a step change in capabilities with autonomous multi-system execution. Anthropic is warning officials about its cybersecurity implications.

2. Claude Code Security Vulnerabilities Stack Up After Source Leak — CVE-2026-21852 let malicious repos steal API keys before trust confirmation. A separate 50+ subcommand bypass evades security analysis.

3. Agent Skills Ecosystem Explodes Past 2,300 Skills — Together AI open-sourced 12 agent skills for Claude Code and Codex. Community marketplaces now list 2,300+ skills and 770+ MCP servers.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 4, 2026
item.description:
1. Anthropic Cuts Off OpenClaw from Claude Subscriptions — Effective today at 12pm PT, third-party harnesses no longer run on Claude subscription credits. Anthropic offers a one-time credit and bundle discounts.

2. Message Batches API Now Supports 300k Output Tokens — Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 unlock 300k output via new beta header. Web search and web fetch graduate out of beta.

3. 1M Context Window Retiring April 30 for Older Models — Claude Sonnet 4.5 and Claude Sonnet 4 lose 1M context on April 30. Migrate to Sonnet 4.6 or Opus 4.6 now.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 3, 2026
item.description:
1. Anthropic Maps 171 Emotion-like Concepts Inside Claude — The interpretability team found internal representations that function like emotions and causally influence Claude's decisions.

2. Trojanized Leak Repos Spreading Vidar Malware — Fake Claude Code source repos on GitHub are delivering infostealers and proxy malware. Hundreds of forks before detection.

3. Usage Limits Apology Fuels More Backlash — Anthropic tried to explain why Pro users hit limits faster. The community response made things worse.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 2, 2026
item.description:
1. Congressman Gottheimer Demands Answers on Leaks — A House Democrat is pressing Anthropic on the Claude Code source leak, Mythos safety risks, and susceptibility to foreign cyberattacks.

2. Claw-Code Hits 100K Stars in One Day — A clean-room Rust rewrite of Claude Code's architecture is now the fastest-growing repo in GitHub history.

3. Claude Buddy Easter Egg Goes Live — Found in the leaked source: an April Fools terminal pet with 18 species, RPG stats, and a 1% shiny rate. Live through April 7.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — April 1, 2026
item.description:
1. Claude Code Source Code Leaked via npm — A packaging error exposed ~500K lines across ~1,900 files. The GitHub backup was forked 41,500+ times. Second major leak in eight days.

2. Claude Code v2.1.89 Ships Same Day — New release adds deferred hook permissions, named subagents for @mention typeahead, and non-blocking MCP connection mode.

3. 1M Token Beta Retiring April 30 — The context-1m beta header stops working for Sonnet 4.5 and Claude 4 at month's end. Migrate to Sonnet 4.6 or Opus 4.6 now.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 31, 2026
item.description:
1. Microsoft Pairs GPT + Claude in Copilot Critique — New feature lets GPT draft research responses while Claude reviews for accuracy. Scores 13.8% higher on DRACO than either model alone.

2. Claude Paid Subs More Than Double in 2026 — Credit card data confirms record subscriber growth. Super Bowl ads, Claude Code, and the Pentagon feud all fueled the surge.

3. Anthropic's Madcap March Wraps Up — 14+ launches, 5 outages, a court victory, an IPO filing in the works, a model leak, and an OSS program. The most consequential month in Anthropic's history ends today.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 30, 2026
item.description:
1. Anthropic Launches "Claude for Open Source" — Free Max 20x access for up to 10,000 qualified OSS maintainers and contributors. Applications open through June 30.

2. Mythos Cyber Fears Hit Washington — Axios reports Anthropic is privately warning top government officials that Mythos makes large-scale cyberattacks significantly more likely in 2026.

3. Pentagon Appeal Clock: 3 Days Left — DOJ has until ~April 2 to appeal Judge Lin's injunction. Legal experts widely expect a Ninth Circuit filing.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 29, 2026
item.description:
1. Anthropic Weighs October IPO at $60B+ Valuation — Bloomberg reports early talks with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley. Racing OpenAI to go public first, with Mythos as the headline product.

2. Claude Paid Subs More Than Double — 1M Signing Up Daily — Daily signups quadrupled from 250K to over 1M since January. Super Bowl ads and Claude Code are the primary growth drivers.

3. Pentagon Has One Week to Appeal Injunction — Judge Lin delayed implementation by seven days. If no appeal is filed by ~April 2, the supply-chain risk designation stays frozen.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 28, 2026
item.description:
1. Mythos Training Complete — Capybara Tier Details Emerge — Anthropic reportedly finishes training Claude Mythos. New details surface on a 10-trillion parameter "Capybara" tier with dramatically higher benchmark scores in cybersecurity and coding.

2. Accenture Launches Cyber.AI Powered by Claude — The enterprise security platform cuts scan turnaround from days to under an hour. API test coverage jumped from 10% to 80% at Accenture's own infrastructure.

3. MCP Hits 97M Monthly Downloads — Moves to Linux Foundation — Anthropic donates MCP governance to the Agentic AI Foundation, co-founded with OpenAI, Block, Google, and Microsoft. New peak-hour usage limits also take effect today.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 27, 2026
item.description:
1. Claude Mythos Leaked — Anthropic's Most Powerful Model Accidentally Revealed — A CMS misconfiguration exposed nearly 3,000 unpublished assets including drafts for "Claude Mythos," a next-gen model outperforming Opus in cybersecurity, coding, and reasoning.

2. Anthropic Weighs October IPO at $60B+ Valuation — Bloomberg reports early talks with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley. Racing OpenAI to go public first.

3. Judge Blocks Pentagon Ban — Calls It First Amendment Retaliation — Judge Rita Lin granted the injunction, suspending the supply-chain risk designation and calling it an "Orwellian notion."

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 26, 2026
item.description:
1. Claude Hit by 3.5-Hour Outage — Opus 4.6 and Cowork Hardest Hit — Yesterday's outage peaked at 5,300+ Downdetector reports. Anthropic identified the root cause and deployed a fix. Service fully restored.

2. Pentagon Ruling Imminent — Anthropic Requested Decision by Today — Judge Rita Lin called the ban "troubling" and said she'd rule within days. Meanwhile, Pentagon CIO confirmed Claude is being used in Operation Epic Fury.

3. Economic Index: Experienced Users 10% More Successful — Anthropic's March report shows 6+ month users do harder work and get better results. Each year of usage correlates with tackling more complex tasks.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 25, 2026
item.description:
1. Judge Says Pentagon Blacklist Looks Like "An Attempt to Cripple" Anthropic — After yesterday's hearing, Judge Rita Lin questioned whether the DOD violated the law. She expects to issue her preliminary injunction ruling within days.

2. Claude Code Auto Mode Ships — Safety Classifier Guards Every Action — A new permissions mode where Claude decides autonomously, with a per-action classifier checking for destructive actions and prompt injection before each tool call runs. Team plan now, Enterprise soon.

3. Claude Code Channels: Text Your AI Coder from Telegram or Discord — Push tasks into a running Claude Code session from your phone. Full filesystem and MCP access. Replies when done. No open ports required.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 24, 2026
item.description:
1. Computer Use Ships for Cowork and Claude Code — Claude can now control your Mac desktop autonomously. Open apps, click, type, navigate browsers. Available to Pro and Max subscribers on macOS. Dispatch lets you assign tasks from your phone.

2. Pentagon Injunction Hearing Today at 1:30pm PST — Judge Rita Lin hears oral arguments in San Francisco. First judicial test of whether the Pentagon's supply-chain risk designation is lawful or unconstitutional retaliation.

3. Ramp AI Index: Nearly 1 in 4 Businesses Now Pay for Anthropic — Adoption hit 24.4% with 4.9% MoM growth. Anthropic wins 70% of head-to-head matchups against OpenAI among first-time buyers. OpenAI saw its biggest monthly decline.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 23, 2026
item.description:
1. Claude Code Channels Ships as Research Preview — Text Claude Code from Discord or Telegram and get replies when it finishes. Plugin runs locally, no open ports. Anthropic's direct answer to OpenClaw. Requires v2.1.80+ and a paid plan.

2. DOJ Calls Pentagon Blacklist "Lawful and Reasonable" — Hearing Tomorrow — Opposition brief filed ahead of Tuesday's 1:30pm PST injunction hearing before Judge Rita Lin. Anthropic fired back in a reply brief calling the designation preemptive retaliation.

3. Claude.ai Login Outage Resolved; 2x Promo Ends March 27 — Sunday's auth issues are cleared. Four days left on the off-peak 2x usage boost for Free, Pro, Max, and Team plans.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 22, 2026
item.description:
1. Anthropic Denies Kill Switch Exists as Monday's Hearing Approaches — Sworn court declaration states Anthropic has no back door, no remote kill switch, and no ability to alter Claude once deployed in DoD systems. Judge Rita Lin hears the injunction case on March 24.

2. Microsoft & 22 Retired Generals Back Anthropic; DoD Claims 6-Month Transition — Former service secretaries and flag officers call the Pentagon designation "retribution." Military users on the ground say replacing Claude is far harder than leadership admits.

3. Extended Thinking Display Field Ships; 2x Usage Promo Ends March 27 — Developers can now omit thinking blocks for faster streaming with thinking.display: "omitted". The off-peak 2x usage boost for Free, Pro, Max, and Team plans has five days left.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 21, 2026
item.description:
1. Pentagon's Own Emails Undercut Its Case Before Monday's Hearing — New court filing reveals DoD told Anthropic they were "very close" on March 4 -- the same day the Pentagon finalized its supply-chain risk designation. Hearing before Judge Rita Lin is Monday.

2. Claude Code Channels Launches as Research Preview — Connect Telegram or Discord to a running Claude Code session and control it from anywhere. Plugin runs locally, no open ports, no public webhook. Requires v2.1.80+ and a paid plan.

3. 1M Context Window Now GA at Standard Pricing — Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 get the full 1M token context window at standard rates -- no more 2x premium. Media cap raised to 600 images or PDF pages per request.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 20, 2026
item.description:
1. DoD Claims It Can Replace Claude in 6 Months — Pentagon CTO says they're "pretty confident" they can phase out Claude by fall 2026, pointing to active OpenAI and Gemini deployments. March 24 court hearing will be decisive.

2. Claude Code Voice Mode & /loop Fully Rolling Out — Push-to-talk voice mode is expanding to all users, alongside the /loop command for recurring task automation and new /effort and /batch controls.

3. 2x Usage Promo Enters Final Week — Anthropic's bonus usage promotion doubles limits for Free, Pro, Max, and Team plans during off-peak hours through March 27.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 19, 2026
item.description:
1. Hegseth Demands Pentagon Fully Drop Claude — Defense Secretary pushes for complete removal from DoD systems, but military users say Claude is embedded in classified workflows and can't be swapped overnight. March 24 court date looms.

2. "Claudy Day" Vulnerability Chain Disclosed — Three chained flaws in Claude.ai enable silent data exfiltration and malicious redirects. Primary injection flaw patched; two mitigations still in progress.

3. Claude Captures 40% of Enterprise LLM Spend — Business subscriptions up 4.9% MoM while OpenAI fell 1.5%. Claude now holds ~40% of enterprise AI spend, up from 24% a year ago.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 18, 2026
item.description:
1. AWS Shifts Defense Workloads Off Claude — Amazon confirms it is transitioning Pentagon-related workloads to alternative models after the DoD supply chain risk designation. Non-defense AWS customers are unaffected.

2. Cowork Gets Persistent Agent Threads — Anthropic begins rolling out persistent agent thread support in Cowork for Max plans today, with Pro plan access following within 48 hours.

3. Claude Code Voice Mode Expands to 5% of Users — Push-to-talk voice coding is live for an initial slice of Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers. Hold spacebar to speak, release to send — now in 20 languages.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 17, 2026
item.description:
1. Claude Partner Network Launches with $100M Investment — Anthropic unveils a structured partner program for enterprises, with Deloitte and Accenture as founding members. The partner team is growing 5x and a new Claude Certified Architect certification is now available.

2. Anthropic Acquires Vercept to Supercharge Computer Use — The 9-person AI perception startup joins Anthropic. Claude already hit 72.5% on OSWorld, up from under 15% when computer use launched in late 2024.

3. Third Claude Outage This Month Hits Free Users — Service disruption on March 17 appears limited to free-tier users on Claude.ai. Anthropic has identified the issue and is deploying a fix.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 15, 2026
item.description:
1. Anthropic Doubles Usage Limits Through March 27 — All Claude users get 2x rate limits during off-peak hours (outside weekdays 8am-2pm ET). Weekends are 2x all day. Free, Pro, Max, Team — everyone benefits automatically.

2. Palmer Luckey: Pentagon Should Have Been "More Forceful" — Anduril founder told Axios that private companies shouldn't dictate how the military uses their tech, backing the Pentagon's blacklist decision.

3. US Army Awards Anduril $20B Contract — A 10-year deal covering autonomous systems, counter-drone tech, and AI-powered command and control. Announced the same week Luckey backed the blacklist.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 16, 2026
item.description:
1. Anthropic Doubles Claude Usage Limits Through March 27 — All tiers get 2x limits outside weekday peak hours. Applies across Claude.ai, Cowork, Claude Code, and the Office add-ins. No action needed.

2. Judge Fast-Tracks Pentagon Hearing to March 24 — Anthropic warned the court billions in revenue are at stake. Dozens of AI researchers from OpenAI and Google filed a letter supporting Anthropic's position.

3. Anthropic Revenue Reportedly Approaching $20B ARR — Nearly doubled from $9B at end of 2025. Enterprise share of AI spending hit 40%, up from 4% a year ago. OpenAI's share fell from 50% to 27%.

Read full report →

item.title: Claude AI Daily Brief — March 14, 2026
item.description:
1. 1M Context Window Goes GA at Standard Pricing — Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 now support 1M tokens with no premium. Media limit jumps to 600 images/PDFs per request. Available on all major clouds today.

2. Pentagon Memo Sets 180-Day Deadline to Remove Claude from Military Systems — Internal DoD memo orders commanders to strip Anthropic products. Claude is currently the only AI deployed on classified Pentagon systems.

3. Palantir Defies Pentagon Blacklist, Keeps Using Claude — Defense contractor maintains its Claude integration despite the supply chain designation, highlighting how deeply embedded Anthropic's models are in military ops.

Read full report →
